userStories.title: 9.1.14 — Refactor PreparationManager and StatusEffectManager to use Entity.buffs
userStories.description:
Tests: unit only — Migrate internal storage from private maps in managers to the Entity.buffs map added in MFG-50.
Context: MFG-50 added buffs: Map<string, number> to Entity with addStacks/getStacks/clearStacks/clearAll methods and tested that managers CAN use them. But the managers themselves were NOT refactored — they still own private maps internally.
Current state:
- src/systems/PreparationManager.ts owns a private Map<string, Map<PreparationType, number>> keyed by entity ID
- src/systems/StatusEffectManager.ts owns a private Map<string, number> for burn stacks, external to the entity
- Entity.buffs exists but is not used by either manager in production code
- MFG-50 tests demonstrate the desired pattern (manager writes to entity.buffs) but the actual manager code hasn't been updated
Required changes:
1. Refactor src/systems/PreparationManager.ts:- Remove the private stacks map
- Use entity.addStacks(prepType, count) instead of writing to internal map
- Use entity.getStacks(prepType) instead of reading from internal map
- Use entity.clearStacks(prepType) for clearing specific preps
- Use entity.clearAll() for interruption (damage, defensive reaction, unrelated action)
- Retain rule logic: maxStacks enforcement, paired action validation, interruption rules
- The manager needs to receive the Entity instance (or look it up) instead of just an entity ID string
2. Refactor src/systems/StatusEffectManager.ts:- Remove the private burn stacks map
- Use entity.addStacks('burn', count) / entity.getStacks('burn') / entity.clearStacks('burn')
- Retain resolution logic: end-of-round damage, consumption rules, bleed persistence
- The manager needs to receive the Entity instance for reads/writes
3. Verify all existing tests pass:- features/unit/preparation.feature (8+ scenarios)
- features/unit/status-effects.feature (6+ scenarios)
- features/unit/entity-buffs.feature (8 scenarios)
Acceptance Criteria:
- PreparationManager has NO private stacks map — all stack storage goes through Entity.buffs
- StatusEffectManager has NO private burn/bleed maps — all storage goes through Entity.buffs
- Both managers retain their rule enforcement logic (max stacks, interruption, end-of-round resolution)
- All existing preparation tests pass
- All existing status-effects tests pass
- All existing entity-buffs tests pass
- npm run test passes

userStories.title: 9.1.13 — Move defensive reactions from AttackEffect to ActionPipeline
userStories.description: Tests: unit + integration — Migrate defensive reaction prompting from AttackEffect into the ActionPipeline so all attack-like effects (Attack, Shoot, Cast) share the same reaction flow.

userStories.title: 9.1.12 — Wire CastEffect to spell resolution (Intensity vs Ward)
userStories.description: Tests: unit only — Fix CastEffect to use the spell resolution system instead of physical attack resolution.

userStories.title: 9.1.11 — Delete UnifiedAttackResolver and rewrite tests against action framework
userStories.description: Tests: unit only — Rewrite existing unified-attack tests to use the action framework pipeline from MFG-51 instead of the standalone resolver.
